notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: Service Manager Proxy exposes GetServiceEnvironment and ListServiceEnvironments only for ServiceEnvironment in v65.110.0. The request identity is serviceEnvironmentId plus compartmentId, and the returned identifiers are explicitly not OCIDs.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching ServiceEnvironment resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime contract is narrower than the visible SDK surface: the published kind is bind-existing and observe-only, requires explicit serviceEnvironmentId plus compartmentId, projects state from GetServiceEnvironment, and uses ListServiceEnvironments only to confirm absence when rereads fail.

Repo-authored lifecycle classification stays explicit: BEGIN_* entitlement transitions requeue, stable entitlement states settle success while the raw SDK status remains visible on the CR, and FAILED_* plus TRA_UNKNOWN are terminal failures. Delete is Kubernetes-local cleanup only because the SDK exposes no delete operation.
